mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the prior month reduces discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not coincide with weaker forward inflation pressure and lower policy rates do not improve subsequent index performance.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines but 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 60% of observations, or the effect disappears after controlling for CPIAUCSL, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and slower demand, which should pressure equities lower over the next 5 trading days through earnings revisions and risk-off positioning.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases or if the sign flips after including PAYEMS confirmation.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates while HOUST remains weak, the market reads consumption as resilient but housing as still restrictive, supporting cyclicals and broad equities over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails if retail sales strength is not associated with better forward returns unless housing also improves.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth exceeds its 6-month average while HOUST stays below its 6-month average
failure_condition: If the joint condition of strong RSAFS and weak HOUST does not outperform single-series signals, or if 20-day returns are not positive in at least 60% of triggered windows, reject the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial throughput and stronger real activity, which should lift earnings expectations and S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The claim is falsified if industrial production gains do not translate into forward equity gains during broad expansionary periods.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO is above its 6-month trend, or if Rank IC is persistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above 4.5 percent tightens financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is invalid if high policy rates coexist with disinflation and do not depress forward returns.
failure_condition: If periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show negative 20-day SPX returns at least 60% of the time, or if CPIAUCSL trends fully neutralize the signal, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous pickup in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies genuine aggregate demand acceleration, which should raise corporate revenue expectations and support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if one series rises without the other, because the signal then reflects noise rather than broad demand.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive in triggered expansion windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, a rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS signals weakening payroll income and lower consumption, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The hypothesis is falsified if labor weakening does not precede equity weakness in slowdown phases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls over the same 2-month window
failure_condition: If the joint deterioration in UNRATE and PAYEMS is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power over single-series effects, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST from depressed levels signals housing stabilization and future broad demand improvement, supporting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days during post-slowdown rebounds. The signal is rejected if housing starts recover without subsequent market breadth improvement.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 5% month over month after being below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound triggers are not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of observations, or if the effect is absent after excluding high-volatility months.
